Instructor

Christina Truong
Tech Professional and Educator

Related to this course

  • Exercise Files
  • Certificates

Course details

  • 5h 26m
  • Beginner
  • Released: 8/9/2023
(1,434)
Cascading Style Sheets (CSS) is a stylesheet language that allows you to control the appearance of your webpages. In this hands-on course, instructor Christina Truong demonstrates the concepts that form the foundation of CSS, explaining what you need to know to tweak existing CSS and start writing your own with ease. Christina explains how to add colors and other design elements to take your webpages beyond just black text on a white background. She shows how to use selectors, how the box model defines the spacing and sizing of page elements, and how to style text and manage basic layouts with Flexbox and Grid. Learn the basics of working with typography, creating fluid and responsive layouts, and determining when to use the float and position properties. Along the way, test out your know-how with real-world projects that let you practice applying new content and skills.

Skills covered

  • Cascading Style Sheets (CSS)

","$recipeTypes":["com.linkedin.98adf13a1575283f5643e0353e81d914"],"$type":"com.linkedin.learning.api.deco.content.TranscriptLine"},{"transcriptStartAt":42007,"caption":" So I'll just add it here. ","$recipeTypes":["com.linkedin.98adf13a1575283f5643e0353e81d914"],"$type":"com.linkedin.learning.api.deco.content.TranscriptLine"},{"transcriptStartAt":51002,"caption":" And this isn't required, ","$recipeTypes":["com.linkedin.98adf13a1575283f5643e0353e81d914"],"$type":"com.linkedin.learning.api.deco.content.TranscriptLine"},{"transcriptStartAt":52008,"caption":" but I'm just going to add a little note here, ","$recipeTypes":["com.linkedin.98adf13a1575283f5643e0353e81d914"],"$type":"com.linkedin.learning.api.deco.content.TranscriptLine"},{"transcriptStartAt":55005,"caption":" just to explain what this line is doing. ","$recipeTypes":["com.linkedin.98adf13a1575283f5643e0353e81d914"],"$type":"com.linkedin.learning.api.deco.content.TranscriptLine"},{"transcriptStartAt":58008,"caption":" Setting the default to 10 pixels. ","$recipeTypes":["com.linkedin.98adf13a1575283f5643e0353e81d914"],"$type":"com.linkedin.learning.api.deco.content.TranscriptLine"},{"transcriptStartAt":65009,"caption":" Now for the base body font, ","$recipeTypes":["com.linkedin.98adf13a1575283f5643e0353e81d914"],"$type":"com.linkedin.learning.api.deco.content.TranscriptLine"},{"transcriptStartAt":67005,"caption":" I'd like to make mine a little bit smaller than the default, ","$recipeTypes":["com.linkedin.98adf13a1575283f5643e0353e81d914"],"$type":"com.linkedin.learning.api.deco.content.TranscriptLine"},{"transcriptStartAt":70008,"caption":" so using the body selector, ","$recipeTypes":["com.linkedin.98adf13a1575283f5643e0353e81d914"],"$type":"com.linkedin.learning.api.deco.content.TranscriptLine"},{"transcriptStartAt":72006,"caption":" I'm going to set the font size to 1.5 rem, ","$recipeTypes":["com.linkedin.98adf13a1575283f5643e0353e81d914"],"$type":"com.linkedin.learning.api.deco.content.TranscriptLine"},{"transcriptStartAt":77008,"caption":" (keyboard clicks) ","$recipeTypes":["com.linkedin.98adf13a1575283f5643e0353e81d914"],"$type":"com.linkedin.learning.api.deco.content.TranscriptLine"},{"transcriptStartAt":81005,"caption":" so this will be equivalent to 15 pixels. ","$recipeTypes":["com.linkedin.98adf13a1575283f5643e0353e81d914"],"$type":"com.linkedin.learning.api.deco.content.TranscriptLine"},{"transcriptStartAt":85005,"caption":" Next, I'll add the line-height property. ","$recipeTypes":["com.linkedin.98adf13a1575283f5643e0353e81d914"],"$type":"com.linkedin.learning.api.deco.content.TranscriptLine"},{"transcriptStartAt":96002,"caption":" (keyboard clicks) ","$recipeTypes":["com.linkedin.98adf13a1575283f5643e0353e81d914"],"$type":"com.linkedin.learning.api.deco.content.TranscriptLine"},{"transcriptStartAt":99009,"caption":" This will make it relative to the font size. ","$recipeTypes":["com.linkedin.98adf13a1575283f5643e0353e81d914"],"$type":"com.linkedin.learning.api.deco.content.TranscriptLine"},{"transcriptStartAt":269007,"caption":" Even though the margin has been removed ","$recipeTypes":["com.linkedin.98adf13a1575283f5643e0353e81d914"],"$type":"com.linkedin.learning.api.deco.content.TranscriptLine"},{"transcriptStartAt":271007,"caption":" we can still see some space in between the headings, ","$recipeTypes":["com.linkedin.98adf13a1575283f5643e0353e81d914"],"$type":"com.linkedin.learning.api.deco.content.TranscriptLine"},{"transcriptStartAt":275004,"caption":" this is coming from the line height ","$recipeTypes":["com.linkedin.98adf13a1575283f5643e0353e81d914"],"$type":"com.linkedin.learning.api.deco.content.TranscriptLine"},{"transcriptStartAt":277001,"caption":" which is relative to the font size. ","$recipeTypes":["com.linkedin.98adf13a1575283f5643e0353e81d914"],"$type":"com.linkedin.learning.api.deco.content.TranscriptLine"},{"transcriptStartAt":279001,"caption":" So sometimes when you're using a large font size ","$recipeTypes":["com.linkedin.98adf13a1575283f5643e0353e81d914"],"$type":"com.linkedin.learning.api.deco.content.TranscriptLine"},{"transcriptStartAt":281008,"caption":" it may result in too much space. ","$recipeTypes":["com.linkedin.98adf13a1575283f5643e0353e81d914"],"$type":"com.linkedin.learning.api.deco.content.TranscriptLine"},{"transcriptStartAt":284008,"caption":" But some space between lines is recommended ","$recipeTypes":["com.linkedin.98adf13a1575283f5643e0353e81d914"],"$type":"com.linkedin.learning.api.deco.content.TranscriptLine"},{"transcriptStartAt":287002,"caption":" to make it easier to read. ","$recipeTypes":["com.linkedin.98adf13a1575283f5643e0353e81d914"],"$type":"com.linkedin.learning.api.deco.content.TranscriptLine"},{"transcriptStartAt":289000,"caption":" Since the H1 is the largest font size ","$recipeTypes":["com.linkedin.98adf13a1575283f5643e0353e81d914"],"$type":"com.linkedin.learning.api.deco.content.TranscriptLine"},{"transcriptStartAt":292002,"caption":" I'll add the line height property ","$recipeTypes":["com.linkedin.98adf13a1575283f5643e0353e81d914"],"$type":"com.linkedin.learning.api.deco.content.TranscriptLine"},{"transcriptStartAt":293007,"caption":" to just this heading and set it to one. ","$recipeTypes":["com.linkedin.98adf13a1575283f5643e0353e81d914"],"$type":"com.linkedin.learning.api.deco.content.TranscriptLine"},{"transcriptStartAt":298002,"caption":" Now the headings are closer together, ","$recipeTypes":["com.linkedin.98adf13a1575283f5643e0353e81d914"],"$type":"com.linkedin.learning.api.deco.content.TranscriptLine"},{"transcriptStartAt":300002,"caption":" but if you want the space to be even closer ","$recipeTypes":["com.linkedin.98adf13a1575283f5643e0353e81d914"],"$type":"com.linkedin.learning.api.deco.content.TranscriptLine"},{"transcriptStartAt":302006,"caption":" use a decimal value smaller than one. ","$recipeTypes":["com.linkedin.98adf13a1575283f5643e0353e81d914"],"$type":"com.linkedin.learning.api.deco.content.TranscriptLine"},{"transcriptStartAt":305005,"caption":" Let's try 0.9. ","$recipeTypes":["com.linkedin.98adf13a1575283f5643e0353e81d914"],"$type":"com.linkedin.learning.api.deco.content.TranscriptLine"},{"transcriptStartAt":308007,"caption":" (keyboard clicks) ","$recipeTypes":["com.linkedin.98adf13a1575283f5643e0353e81d914"],"$type":"com.linkedin.learning.api.deco.content.TranscriptLine"},{"transcriptStartAt":310003,"caption":" We could even try 0.8, ","$recipeTypes":["com.linkedin.98adf13a1575283f5643e0353e81d914"],"$type":"com.linkedin.learning.api.deco.content.TranscriptLine"},{"transcriptStartAt":313006,"caption":" or we can get even more granular and go with 0.85. ","$recipeTypes":["com.linkedin.98adf13a1575283f5643e0353e81d914"],"$type":"com.linkedin.learning.api.deco.content.TranscriptLine"},{"transcriptStartAt":319009,"caption":" Now the headings are closer together ","$recipeTypes":["com.linkedin.98adf13a1575283f5643e0353e81d914"],"$type":"com.linkedin.learning.api.deco.content.TranscriptLine"},{"transcriptStartAt":321006,"caption":" and the H3 headings in the project area ","$recipeTypes":["com.linkedin.98adf13a1575283f5643e0353e81d914"],"$type":"com.linkedin.learning.api.deco.content.TranscriptLine"},{"transcriptStartAt":324003,"caption":" are almost lined up to the image. ","$recipeTypes":["com.linkedin.98adf13a1575283f5643e0353e81d914"],"$type":"com.linkedin.learning.api.deco.content.TranscriptLine"},{"transcriptStartAt":327003,"caption":" There's still a little bit of space at the top, ","$recipeTypes":["com.linkedin.98adf13a1575283f5643e0353e81d914"],"$type":"com.linkedin.learning.api.deco.content.TranscriptLine"},{"transcriptStartAt":330000,"caption":" and again that's coming from the line height. ","$recipeTypes":["com.linkedin.98adf13a1575283f5643e0353e81d914"],"$type":"com.linkedin.learning.api.deco.content.TranscriptLine"},{"transcriptStartAt":333001,"caption":" But I actually don't mind the space here ","$recipeTypes":["com.linkedin.98adf13a1575283f5643e0353e81d914"],"$type":"com.linkedin.learning.api.deco.content.TranscriptLine"},{"transcriptStartAt":335002,"caption":" so I'm going to leave that as is. ","$recipeTypes":["com.linkedin.98adf13a1575283f5643e0353e81d914"],"$type":"com.linkedin.learning.api.deco.content.TranscriptLine"},{"transcriptStartAt":338001,"caption":" We've just tested a bunch of styles in the dev tools, ","$recipeTypes":["com.linkedin.98adf13a1575283f5643e0353e81d914"],"$type":"com.linkedin.learning.api.deco.content.TranscriptLine"},{"transcriptStartAt":340004,"caption":" but remember, changes here aren't actually applied ","$recipeTypes":["com.linkedin.98adf13a1575283f5643e0353e81d914"],"$type":"com.linkedin.learning.api.deco.content.TranscriptLine"},{"transcriptStartAt":343005,"caption":" to the CSS file, so let's add that in. ","$recipeTypes":["com.linkedin.98adf13a1575283f5643e0353e81d914"],"$type":"com.linkedin.learning.api.deco.content.TranscriptLine"},{"transcriptStartAt":347000,"caption":" Back in the CSS file, we'll add the font weight ","$recipeTypes":["com.linkedin.98adf13a1575283f5643e0353e81d914"],"$type":"com.linkedin.learning.api.deco.content.TranscriptLine"},{"transcriptStartAt":351001,"caption":" set to 400 to remove the default for all the headings. ","$recipeTypes":["com.linkedin.98adf13a1575283f5643e0353e81d914"],"$type":"com.linkedin.learning.api.deco.content.TranscriptLine"},{"transcriptStartAt":357008,"caption":" Then set the margin to zero to remove ","$recipeTypes":["com.linkedin.98adf13a1575283f5643e0353e81d914"],"$type":"com.linkedin.learning.api.deco.content.TranscriptLine"},{"transcriptStartAt":360007,"caption":" the default margin space for all the headings as well. ","$recipeTypes":["com.linkedin.98adf13a1575283f5643e0353e81d914"],"$type":"com.linkedin.learning.api.deco.content.TranscriptLine"},{"transcriptStartAt":364009,"caption":" And for the H1, I chose to add a line height value of 0.85. ","$recipeTypes":["com.linkedin.98adf13a1575283f5643e0353e81d914"],"$type":"com.linkedin.learning.api.deco.content.TranscriptLine"},{"transcriptStartAt":371003,"caption":" (keyboard clicks) ","$recipeTypes":["com.linkedin.98adf13a1575283f5643e0353e81d914"],"$type":"com.linkedin.learning.api.deco.content.TranscriptLine"},{"transcriptStartAt":374004,"caption":" Now let's save the file and go back to the browser ","$recipeTypes":["com.linkedin.98adf13a1575283f5643e0353e81d914"],"$type":"com.linkedin.learning.api.deco.content.TranscriptLine"},{"transcriptStartAt":377005,"caption":" and make sure we captured all the updates. ","$recipeTypes":["com.linkedin.98adf13a1575283f5643e0353e81d914"],"$type":"com.linkedin.learning.api.deco.content.TranscriptLine"},{"transcriptStartAt":384003,"caption":" Let's also check out the homepage. gApiSocialWatchersSummary:urn:li:learningApiCourse:41979153","objectives":[],"*authorsV2":["urn:li:learningApiAuthor:3891839"],"cachingKey":"urn:li:learningApiCourse:41979153","descriptionV3":{"attributes":[],"text":"Cascading Style Sheets (CSS) is a stylesheet language that allows you to control the appearance of your webpages. In this hands-on course, instructor Christina Truong demonstrates the concepts that form the foundation of CSS, explaining what you need to know to tweak existing CSS and start writing your own with ease. Christina explains how to add colors and other design elements to take your webpages beyond just black text on a white background. She shows how to use selectors, how the box model defines the spacing and sizing of page elements, and how to style text and manage basic layouts with Flexbox and Grid. Learn the basics of working with typography, creating fluid and responsive layouts, and determining when to use the float and position properties. Along the way, test out your know-how with real-world projects that let you practice applying new content and skills.","$recipeTypes":["com.linkedin.2f74bab981f1cb6943706f82ef36bfde"],"$type":"com.linkedin.learning.api.deco.common.AttributedTextModel"},"externalProviderDetails":null,"*activityTransferConsentStatusV2":"urn:li:la_activityTransferConsentStatus:AEQAAA0bgCUBk01xMpZk5ubc5dXJ7uAmt0N_t0U"},{"*bookmark":"urn:li:learningApiBookmark:urn:li:learningApiVideo:48033800","visibility":"SUBSCRIBED","entityType":"VIDEO","trackingUrn":"urn:li:lyndaVideo:(urn:li:lyndaCourse:4379397,4490944)","title":"The box model
